content.full_text:
RAVI MEUTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
September 1,
1995
Gary L. Wolfe
Vance, Thrift and Biller
181 South Ash Street
P.O.
Box 1268
Ventura,
CA
93002
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-269
Dear Mr. Wolfe:
This is in response to your letter requesting confirmation of
telephone advice provided to Sherry Lions on behalf of the
Committee to Elect Larry Carpenter Sheriff under the Political
Reform Act
(the htAct).L
Please note that this letter is based on
the facts presented to us.
The Commission does not act as the
finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Oglesbv
(1975)
1 FPPC
Ops.
71.)
This letter confirms that your letter dated August 14,
1995,
accurately summarizes the advice I provided to you on that date.
You asked whether a candidate could give up to $250 dollars each
to two campaign workers who were also husband and wife.
You
stated that the gift was intended to honor their 50th wedding
anniversary.
As we discussed Section 89513(f) (1) provides:
Campaign funds shall not be used to make
personal gifts unless the gift is directly related
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000-18995.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 1 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A—95—269
Page
2
to a political, legislative,
or governmental
purpose.
***
[Gjifts with a total cwaulative value of less
than two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
in a single
year made to an individual employee,
a committee
worker,
or an employee of the elected officer’s
agency, are considered to be directly related to a
political, legislative,
or governmental purpose.
For purposes of this paragraph,
a gift to a member
of a person’s immediate family shall be deemed to
be a gift to that person.
Since each of the intended recipients are campaign workers in
their own right, the sheriff may give a gift of up to $250 to each
in a calendar year..
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322_5660.2
Sincerely
Steven G.
Churchwell
al Counsel
John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
2
Copies of Commission -regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of
1Oc per page.

sections.question: You asked whether a candidate could give up to $250 dollars each to two campaign workers who were also husband and wife. You stated that the gift was intended to honor their 50th wedding anniversary.
sections.conclusion: Since each of the intended recipients are campaign workers in their own right, the sheriff may give a gift of up to $250 to each in a calendar year.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate may use campaign funds to give gifts of up to $250 each to two campaign workers (a married couple) as a gift honoring their 50th wedding anniversary.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The sheriff may give gifts of up to $250 to each campaign worker in a calendar year, as gifts under $250 in total cumulative value to individual committee workers are deemed directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose under Government Code Section 89513(f)(1).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmation letter of prior telephone advice. Contains clear question and conclusion sections. Document dated September 1, 1995; File No. A-95-269.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked whether a candidate could give up to $250 dollars each to two campaign workers who were also husband and wife. You stated that the gift was intended to honor their 50th wedding anniversary.

CONCLUSION: Since each of the intended recipients are campaign workers in their own right, the sheriff may give a gift of up to $250 to each in a calendar year.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms advice that a candidate may give up to $250 gifts to each of two campaign workers (married couple) to honor their 50th wedding anniversary, as such gifts are permissible under Government Code Section 89513(f)(1) when recipients are campaign workers.
